text: The surgeon manipulates the thickened gallbladder, which shows signs of active inflammation and adherence to the hepatocolic omentum and duodenum. Despite the inflammation, the surgeon gains access to visualize the base of the gallbladder and the common duct.

The surgeon attempts to enter the fat plane at the bottom of the gallbladder, performing lateral and medial dissection to lift the gallbladder. A line is marked across the bottom of the gallbladder, anticipating the location of the artery. The peritoneum is opened on the medial aspect, while the Firefly imaging system provides limited assistance in identifying the cystic duct due to obstruction.

The surgeon converts to a top-down approach for the cholecystectomy, carefully dissecting within the thickened gallbladder wall. Indocyanine green (ICG) imaging is used to differentiate the liver from the gallbladder tissue, providing clear visual guidance during the dissection.

The surgeon continues the dissection towards the bottom of the gallbladder, performing blunt dissection near the artery. Improved mobility at the gallbladder's base aids in the dissection, revealing the junction into the cystic duct behind a layer of fatty tissue. The surgeon chisels further to expose the bottom of the gallbladder.

The surgeon performs blunt dissection to expose the bottom of the gallbladder, aiming to identify the cystic duct and artery. The gallbladder is being separated from surrounding tissues, with the thickened tissue visible. The surgeon sweeps down other structures to isolate the gallbladder, focusing on the attachment to the liver bed to achieve a critical view for safely addressing the duct and artery.

The surgeon visually identifies the cystic duct and artery, working to create space between them by dissecting posterior tissue. The separation between the artery and duct is achieved, allowing for clipping and division.

The surgeon clears the remaining tissue from the posterior aspect of the cystic duct, leaving the cystic duct exposed. The cystic duct is clipped and divided, with the surgeon inspecting the rest of the biliary tree, including the bifurcation.
